phase_status: JUDGMENT-DAY ROUND 1 FOUND THREE REAL addImport DEFECTS that three verify-final iterations AND the four-persona Council all missed. The blind gate earned its keep a second time. Root cause (one, not three): REQ-RXD-05 step 1's already-bound check had a wrong notion of 'bound' — it compared LOCAL names, ignored type-only-ness, and only considered the SAME module. (1) CRITICAL: `import type { Icon }` + addImport('Icon','./icons') -> silent no-op; Icon never value-bound; erased at compile -> ReferenceError at runtime. EXACTLY the class V5's changelog claimed to close — V5 fixed the MERGE (step 2, which correctly excludes type-only) but step 1 runs FIRST and never reaches it. Both judges' mutation tables independently showed the isTypeOnly guard killing only 1 of ~196 tests (denylist kills 17, reserved-words kills 11) — Judge A chased the weak signal and found the bug; Judge B logged it and moved on. (2) WARNING(real): cross-module duplicate — `import { useState } from 'react'` + addImport('useState','./local') emits code node rejects with SyntaxError: Identifier already declared. REQ-RXD-05's headline says 'MUST NEVER produce an invalid or duplicate binding' UNQUALIFIED, but the algorithm only reasoned about the same module — the spec contradicted itself and the code followed the steps while violating the requirement. (3) WARNING(real): aliased — `import { Foo as x }` + addImport('x','./a') -> silent no-op, every reference to x resolves to Foo. V6 step 1 explicitly SANCTIONED this. ORCHESTRATOR ERROR: I relayed Judge A's claim that `import { Foo as x, x }` is legal. It is NOT — verified myself: it fails at PARSE time (SyntaxError: Identifier 'x' has already been declared), while the control `import { Foo as x, y }` gets past parsing and fails only at module resolution. sdd-spec tested the premise rather than trusting my brief, and that correction COLLAPSED defects 2 and 3 into one mechanism (a claimed local name). It also proved with tsc that the naive type-only fix I suggested (exclude type-only, fall through to CREATE) is itself unsafe: `import type { Icon }` + `import { Icon }` = TS2300 Duplicate identifier.
next_phase: judgment-day Round 1 FIX AGENT against owner-signed V7, then RE-JUDGE both judges in parallel (blind, fresh delegates) per the judgment-day protocol Pattern 4. Fix scope: (a) V7's claimed-name algorithm — the three addImport defects; (b) the 16-char echo truncation with no marker in reject-tail.ts boundedFragment, CONFIRMED by both judges — mainstream React component names exceed 16 chars, so authors get an error naming a component that does not exist in their source; (c) find('.tsx') accepted vs the JSDoc's dotfile claim, CONFIRMED by both judges — the JSDoc ships into the published .d.ts. NOT fixed, register as followups: element-grammar excludes $/namespace/non-ASCII (theoretical, capability gap needing a spec decision); getAttribute first-match on duplicate attributes (theoretical, needs malformed input). Then verify-final iteration 4 -> steward reckoning -> arch audit -> archive.
spec_detail: V7, owner-signed 2026-07-17 — unfrozen from V6-SIGNED after judgment-day. Amends REQ-RXD-05 (unified algorithm) + REQ-RXD-09 (honest-limitations doc). All 15 REQ-IDs survive; 05.1-05.10 and 09.1-09.5 byte-unchanged and hand-traced against the new algorithm — all ten keep their signed outputs. MINTED 05.11-05.15 + 09.6. THE FIX IS ONE IDEA, NOT THREE: a new Step 2 'claimed name' collision check — file-wide, ANY module, value-bound OR type-only — runs after idempotency and before merge/create, and REJECTS via dialectError. Type-only bindings CLAIM their local name (they occupy the identifier slot) even though they do not satisfy the value-binding promise. Pinned at both granularities: 05.11 declaration-level (`import type { X }`), 05.12 inline (`import { type X }`), plus 05.13 boundary proving a differently-aliased type-only import does NOT collide. CROSS-MODULE RULING = REJECT, not narrow-the-wording: rejecting makes the headline invariant literally and unconditionally TRUE on success — the op either honours both promises fully or throws before touching the AST, never a silent partial failure. Mirrors REQ-RXD-04's existing zero/multi-match reject precedent, so it is not a new op philosophy. REQ-RXD-09 touched because success is now conditional — the collision-reject is documented as a guard-asserted honest limitation. Owner endorsed the Defect-3 premise correction at sign time. PRIOR: V6 signed 2026-07-17 (F-1 closure); V5 signed 2026-07-17 (council fix pass); V4 signed 2026-07-16.
verify_final_iter3: Iteration 3 at 9a1c23c: **pass-with-followups — ARCHIVE-READY, and this token is CORRECT, not a shortfall**. ORCHESTRATOR ERROR CORRECTED BY THE VERIFIER: the launch prompt asserted 'archive requires verify-report = pass'. That is FALSE — sdd-archive/SKILL.md:44 accepts BOTH `pass` and `pass-with-followups`, and the token is load-bearing in the OPPOSITE direction: Step 9 (SKILL.md:392-417) registers followups into project/pending-changes ONLY on `pass-with-followups`, and explicitly SKIPS that step on `pass`. Reporting `pass` with F-2..F-8 open would have SILENTLY DROPPED EIGHT tracked followups. The verifier refused the premise and cited the file. Do not 'upgrade' this verdict to `pass` — that would destroy the followup registration. F-1 RESOLVED: mechanical set diff reproduced from the spec's own V6 prose (spec 48 / code 48 / missing [] / extra [] / EXACT MATCH true, both directions); REQ-RXD-06.9 implemented with substring counter-check 5/0; reproduced at the PUBLIC API beyond the validator (addImport('eval'|'arguments') rejects, zero directives, file byte-unchanged; lookalikes accepted and emit). V6 COMPLETENESS CLAIM HOLDS, attacked not nodded: oracle = node's real ES module parser via vm.SourceTextModule; ~194.7M candidates, ZERO disagreements both directions (exhaustive len 1-4 over the full regex alphabet = 14.4M, exhaustive len 5 = 11.9M, len 6 partial 368/676 prefixes = 168M, plus 236k curated covering every ES/TS keyword, contextual keyword and global). No 49th exists. HONEST LIMIT stated by the verifier: len >=7 rests on the curated tier, not exhaustion — the sweeps corroborate the deduction, they do not replace it. TDD PASS: RED reproduced in a scratch worktree by reverting the two entries -> 3 pass / 2 fail, both on toBeInstanceOf(Error) receiving undefined — i.e. the validator not throwing, the defect itself. Not module resolution, not syntax. Step 11b GATING: clean, 0 Bug/Architecture/MAJOR. Suite 1829 pass / 0 fail, 181 files, 20.32s in a fresh /private/tmp worktree, ZERO environmental timeouts.
verify_final_f1_closure: F-1 CLOSED 2026-07-17 against owner-signed V6 — commits 9a92944 (fix) + 299fc32 (docs). IMPORT_RESERVED_WORDS 46 -> 48. Set verified MECHANICALLY, not asserted: the runtime Set was parsed and diffed against the spec's own 48-entry enumeration -> 'spec count: 48, code count: 48, missing: [], extra: [], EXACT MATCH: true'. TDD was a GENUINE RED->GREEN (the values were accepted before, so real RED was available and no probe substituted for it): scoped run pre-fix 3 pass / 2 fail, both failing for the right reason (toBeInstanceOf(Error) receiving undefined); post-fix 5 pass / 0 fail. Suite 1829 pass / 0 fail in a fresh /private/tmp worktree = 1824 baseline + 5 net new, exact, no unexplained delta, no environmental timeout observed. Scope discipline held: only IMPORT_RESERVED_WORDS + the REQ-RXD-06.9 test touched. Iteration 3 re-verify pending to convert pass-with-followups -> pass.
verify_final_detail: Iteration 2 at 01ecec7 (verify-report.md, overwritten — iteration 1's fail at f12ffba is preserved as history inside it). All NINE routed council items RESOLVED, each independently reproduced rather than trusted. Step 11b gating audit: no Bug/Architecture/MAJOR. Finding categories: quality (F-1, F-2, F-5), process (F-3), design-conformance (F-4) — none gating. Suite 1824 pass / 0 fail in a fresh /private/tmp worktree; tsc 0 errors; build exit 0; NO environmental timeout observed. TDD Cycle Adherence: PASS — the near-miss-mutant amendment is HONOURED and the verifier reproduced BOTH legs himself (baseline 76/0; mutant + old assertion 76/0 = false pass; mutant + new assertion 65/11 = killed). The apply record RETRACTS its own false discrimination claim in place rather than quietly editing it — the correct disposal. The disclosed 5749bf2 commit-message deviation ruled RECOVERABLE: it misleads about which commit to read, never about what shipped. adversarial_review: REQUIRED (L + sensitive) — judgment-day BLIND has NOT yet run.
verify_final_open_decision: F-1 (owner's call, NOT gating): addImport('arguments') and addImport('eval') emit code that node rejects with `SyntaxError: Unexpected eval or arguments in strict mode`. Same class as SEC-1, narrowed to 2 identifiers. SPEC-CONFORMANT — REQ-RXD-06 says 'at minimum' and these are not reserved *words* — so the verifier reported rather than routed. But it sits in the channel the spec itself titles 'security — load-bearing', and his brute-force sweep proves that closing it makes the denylist PROVABLY COMPLETE at 48/48. Options: sdd-spec V6 -> sdd-apply for zero residual, or register as a pending-change.
carry_forward: REQ-RXD-11.5 wording amendment -> pending-changes at archive.

Strict TDD ordering violation on S-002 (impl written same editing pass as tests; RED proven retroactively) is DISCLOSED, not resolved — sdd-verify --mode=final MUST audit it in TDD Cycle Adherence. Do not let it downgrade to clean by attrition.

RESOLVED 2026-07-17 by diagnostic — full-suite instability is ENVIRONMENTAL, not a regression and not parallelism. The 'parallel-execution flakiness' hypothesis is REFUTED: bun test is sequential by default and package.json:64 runs bare `bun test` with no flags; nothing was ever parallel. Control experiment: HEAD's exact code (6ee9af0) checked out into /private/tmp gives 1779 pass / 0 fail, 3/3 runs (~23s) — so apply-progress.md's '1779/0' claim is truthful and exactly reproducible, just not under ~/Documents. Mechanism: subprocess-bound tests (bun install / bun link / tsc --noEmit via blocking spawnSync) run against bun's default 5000ms timeout while first-touch file scanning under ~/Documents inflates the first `tsc --noEmit` from 663ms to 16694ms (26x). tsc --extendedDiagnostics proves identical work both sides (256 files / 48335 symbols / 556 types). Strong inference: Microsoft Defender (wdavdaemon) real-time scan; NOT A/B-proven (corporate EDR cannot be disabled to confirm). Ruled out: parallelism, regression, ts-morph/type-graph growth, dist/testing/commons d.ts drift, core AST leakage (FIT-37 holds), iCloud sync, port binding, process.cwd() mutation. NO shared-state defect exists.

OPEN (pre-existing debt, NOT react-dialect scope): subprocess-bound tests declare no explicit timeout — test/docs/quickstart-docs.test.ts:312 and :319, plus the S-03 permissive-proof CI guard and the installed-consumer-vantage e2e leg. Their measured work is ~2s even on a fast filesystem against a 5000ms budget; the declared budget does not describe the work regardless of Defender. Fix = explicit per-test timeouts (~30 min). Do NOT raise the global timeout (masks genuinely slow tests). Environment-level companion fix (not committed): Defender real-time-scan exclusion for the repo or at minimum .tmp-*/ scratch dirs.
